title: orthosiphon welwitschii var. angolensis
intro: Orthosiphon welwitschii var. angolensis, also known as Uguha or African Orthosiphon, is a plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family. It is traditionally used in African and Asian cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly for its diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to provide several health benefits, including the relief of urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and hypertension, as well as the treatment of rheumatism and other inflammatory conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Orthosiphon welwitschii var. angolensis are often dried and made into teas, infusions, or capsules to harness its medicinal properties.
